user_query:
I need a full static security assessment of the skill folder at input/skill-scaaner/. Please scan SKILL.md and all .py files recursively and produce two deliverables:

1) Write a machine-checkable JSON report to output/security_findings.json with this schema exactly:
{
  "skill": "<directory name>",
  "total_findings": <int>,
  "findings": [
    {"file": "<relative path>", "line": <int or 0>, "severity": "CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW", "description": "<short rationale>", "match": "<evidence snippet>"}
  ],
  "by_severity": {"CRITICAL": <int>, "HIGH": <int>, "MEDIUM": <int>, "LOW": <int>},
  "verdict": "CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM|CLEAN"
}
Rules for classification:
- Use CRITICAL for dynamic code execution (e.g., eval/exec), fetch-and-execute patterns, or explicit prompt-injection override lines.
- Use HIGH for shell execution (including shell=True), POST exfiltration, credential/file harvesting, path traversal, or typosquatting.
- Use MEDIUM for base64 decoding and logging of env/config, compiled/asset risks, permissions that are excessive, or similar.
- LOW for minor concerns like non-HTTPS links.
Also include evidence snippets in the "match" field and do not execute any code.

2) Write a remediation plan to output/remediation_plan.md that prioritizes fixes by severity. Include sections for Critical, High, Medium, and Low with concrete steps (e.g., remove eval, strip POST exfiltration, enforce HTTPS, reduce env var requests, fix typosquatting by renaming) and rationale.

Scope and expectations:
- Identify and classify items such as: prompt-injection phrasing like "ignore previous instructions" in SKILL.md, non-HTTPS links, requests for API keys/tokens, data exfiltration (e.g., requests.post), shell execution with shell=True or os.system, base64.b64decode used with eval, eval(urlopen(...)) fetch-and-execute, logging or printing environment variables, and any path traversal patterns.
- Perform a typosquatting check on the skill directory name against common/popular names; if it appears to be a typosquat, include a HIGH-severity finding mentioning the likely target name and why.
- Aggregate counts per severity in by_severity and set verdict = CRITICAL if any CRITICAL exists; else HIGH if any HIGH; else MEDIUM if any findings; else CLEAN.
- Include line numbers when possible; otherwise use line = 0.

Write only the two files under output/ as specified.
